research_problem.value: The central challenge is how to acquire and utilize high-quality dense rewards at scale, enabling online process reward model (PRM) updates efficiently.
research_problem.evidence: We identify the central challenge as how to acquire and utilize high-quality dense rewards at scale, which enables online process reward model (PRM) update efficiently.

current_limitations.value: Current methods either need complicated human annotation pipelines or rely on estimation-based methods, which require about 10 times more rollouts for each step than sampling only the response-level trajectories.
current_limitations.evidence: Existing methods either need to build complicated human annotation pipelines... or rely on estimation-based methods, which require about $1 0 	imes$ more rollouts for each step than sampling only the response-level trajectories.

key_challenges.value: The key challenges include defining process rewards, scaling online updates for PRMs, and the extra cost of explicit reward modeling.
key_challenges.evidence: C1. Process rewards are hard to define... C2. PRM online updates are not scalable... C3. Explicit reward modeling brings extra cost.

provenance.section_header: 2.2 KEY CHALLENGES IN SCALABLE DENSE REWARDS

main_idea.value: PRIME employs implicit process rewards, which are derivable from the Implicit PRM trained with only outcome labels, enabling online updates to avoid reward hacking.
main_idea.evidence: The key insight of PRIME is to apply implicit process rewards, which are derivable from the Implicit PRM that is trained with only outcome labels.

inspiration_source.value: The theoretical foundation comes from the need to improve the scalability and effectiveness of dense rewards in reinforcement learning for LLMs.
inspiration_source.evidence: This property enables us to update the PRMs online to avoid reward hacking.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

objective.value: The objective is to train dense reward models with only outcome-level labels.
objective.evidence: At its core, the framework employs recently proposed implicit process reward modeling... to train dense reward models with only outcome-level labels.

method.value: The method involves updating the Implicit PRM online with on-policy rollouts and outcome supervision.
method.evidence: In PRIME, upon rollouts being generated and graded... we update the Implicit PRM online with on-policy rollouts and outcome supervision.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

reward_calculation.value: The process rewards are calculated using the formula r_φ(y_t) := β log(π_φ(y_t | y_<t) / π_ref(y_t | y_<t)).
reward_calculation.evidence: The process rewards are obtained by: r _ { \phi } ( y _ { t } ) : =  eta \log  rac { \pi _ { \phi } ( y _ { t } | \mathbf { y } _ { < t } ) } { \pi _ { \mathrm { r e f } } ( y _ { t } | \mathbf { y } _ { < t } )

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

training_objective.value: The training objective involves using cross-entropy loss on the implicit PRM.
training_objective.evidence: Update Implicit PRM by CE loss on (x, y, r_o(y)) ∈ T.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

workflow.value: PRIME combines implicit process rewards with outcome rewards into any kind of advantage estimate.
workflow.evidence: PRIME is illustrated in Figure 2 and Algorithm 1.

key_techniques.value: Key techniques include online updates of the PRM and a flexible framework for advantage estimation.
key_techniques.evidence: We then design a flexible framework to incorporate implicit process rewards with outcome rewards into any kind of MC advantage estimate.

inference_enhancement.value: Inference is enhanced by using implicit rewards to improve the accuracy of predictions.
inference_enhancement.evidence: Online update mitigates overoptimization and reward hacking.

provenance.section_header: 5.1 DESIGN CHOICES FOR THE IMPLICIT PRM

experimental_setup.value: The experiments involved training Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base with PRIME after a lightweight SFT warmup stage.
experimental_setup.evidence: In experiments, we train Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base (Yang et al., 2024b) with PRIME after a lightweight SFT warmup stage.

algorithm_effectiveness.value: PRIME achieves a 15.1% average improvement across several key reasoning benchmarks over the SFT model.
algorithm_effectiveness.evidence: Starting from Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base, PRIME achieves a $1 5 . 1 \%$ average improvement across several key reasoning benchmarks over the SFT model.

acceleration_effect.value: PRIME achieves a 2.5x sample efficiency gain.
acceleration_effect.evidence: Compared to RL using outcome rewards only, PRIME achieves a $2 . 5 	imes$ sample efficiency gain.

online_prm_importance.value: Updating the PRM online is key to the success of PRIME.
online_prm_importance.evidence: Our analysis shows that updating the PRM online is key to the success of PRIME.

inference_scaling_effect.value: PRIME leads to a 6.9% performance improvement on challenging math problems.
inference_scaling_effect.evidence: PRIME achieves a $6 . 9 \%$ performance improvements on challenging math problems.

main_findings.value: PRIME enhances reasoning capabilities via efficient reinforcement learning with dense token-level rewards.
main_findings.evidence: This work develops PRIME, which produces and leverages dense rewards in online RL for LLM reasoning.

technical_contributions.value: The technical contributions include the development of a scalable framework for online updates of PRMs.
technical_contributions.evidence: To address the above challenges, we propose PRIME, a scalable online RL method with dense rewards.

practical_contributions.value: PRIME significantly reduces the development overhead for reinforcement learning models.
practical_contributions.evidence: PRIME combines well with various advantage functions and forgoes the dedicated reward model training phase that existing approaches require, substantially reducing the development overhead.

community_contributions.value: PRIME's methodology is open-sourced, encouraging further research and development in the community.
community_contributions.evidence: The code and models are available on GitHub.
